programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The mother of a model threatens to kill anyone who proposes to her daughter, and becomes the prime suspect when a private investigator is murdered. Lesley-Anne Down guest stars

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio investigates the apparent suicide of an anaesthetist who worked in a cosmetic surgery clinic, only to discover foul play was involved. Investigations lead him to a bereaved widower who believes the victim was responsible for his wife's death

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Abby becomes personally involved in the case of a murdered naval scientist who she regards as a kindred spirit. The team's initial inquiries turn up little concrete information about the victim's life, but the forensic specialist makes a breakthrough when she decodes some of the mysterious markings covering the body. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Brandon Valdivia falls to his death from a rooftop bar and is initially thought to have committed suicide, but his family refuse to accept that possibility and persuade a congressman to put some pressure on the admiral. Sam and Callen are then brought in to re-evaluate the case and discover that Valdivia may have been involved in money laundering with a fellow marine.

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: When tragedy strikes, Richard Kimble risks a visit to his sister Donna in his old hometown though he is certain Gerard won't be far

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: On the eve of her wedding to a rancher, the body of a woman is found in a car boot and Perry must prove that a foreman accused of the murder is innocent

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While performing an autopsy for a class of military students, Ducky discovers the cadaver he is using is the body of a murder victim. In trying to identify the corpse, Gibbs and his team are set on the trail of a career criminal specialising in identity fraud. Meanwhile, Ziva has difficulty separating her personal feelings and professional judgment. Starring David McCallum and Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The Rangers investigate a series of accidents involving a Cherokee Indian racing team. Trent and Carlos save a young girl from a paedophile

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The aftermath of Harper Dearing's bomb attack on the NCIS headquarters dominates proceedings. As rescue crews sift through the debris searching for survivors, Abby, Gibbs, McGee and Vance are accounted for, but Ziva and Tony are trapped in a lift. Dearing makes contact after his ex-wife is taken into custody, enabling his car to be traced to an abandoned lot - and the agents arrive just in time to see the vehicle blow up. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A woman is shot dead in an apartment while keeping watch on a man on a yacht in a marina, and the killer takes her camera and computer. A flash drive is recovered from the victim's baseball cap and surveillance footage on it includes images of Callen, who deduces that the pictures were taken in the days leading up to the attempt on his life and that the dead woman was the last person to see him before he was shot

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy Seal is shot dead while threatening to throw himself from the top of a building. An al-Qaeda operative emerges as the main suspect, but DiNozzo thinks the killer may actually be found closer to home. Elsewhere, a rumour spreads that Abby may be about to leave NCIS for good. Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star in the American drama

programme.title: Birds of a Feather

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey makes an appointment with a Harley Street specialist, hoping to allay her suspicions that she may be pregnant. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Diane encounters an old army colleague and the incident stirs painful memories of her service days when a young soldier committed suicide. Smithy lends a sympathetic ear when she confides in him about her guilty feelings at having failed to halt the bullying which caused the tragedy. Mickey pushes the boat out to capture Mia's heart and Honey invites Will back to her place for the night after an evening in the pub

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric learns a drug-addicted patient was his late son Leo's partner. As Sam gets to know Kieron, he comes to realise what sacrifices his son is prepared to make for their relationship. It is Kyla's last day and Max has a surprise in store for her, while Linden begins to lose faith after spending the night at Callie's bedside. Daisha makes a big decision about baby Joe

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Harry misreads Lara's signals at the calendar launch, leading to an ill-advised attempt to kiss her. Roxy confesses to being a surrogate mother and Comfort is led astray by a friend out for a good time, but a prank involving a stolen car has fatal consequences. Jim gets down on bended knee to propose to Nikki and a paranoid Mike decides to quit when embarrassingly faced with an old colleague

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A Turkish man reports his 16-year-old daughter missing, but further inquiries reveal she is happily living with her boyfriend and has no intention of returning to a life of drudgery and exploitation. Stuart takes over the case when the worried father denounces the boy's family for setting up illegal marriages for immigrants in return for cash. Mickey's old flame Carrie Morgan turns up at Sun Hill accused of theft by her employer, and the harassed detective realises he must distance himself from the ex-prostitute if he wants to make his relationship with Mia work

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sam tries her hand at pulling pints and receives unexpected attention from Ricky. David attempts to turn the tables on Grant and Phil by talking of blackmail

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy lays down the law with her family, while Pat's warnings to David go unheeded. Sam's appearance at the Quiz Night causes tensions to explode, and Carol gets a nasty shock

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George is asked to host a Japanese trade delegation on the eve of Remembrance Day. Knowing how the community will react, he asks Jack to come to the reception in the hope that the approval of a former Japanese prisoner of war might help deal with any ill feeling. At the event Regina is openly hostile toward Sarah, which she attributes to anti-Semitism. Anna's prolonged absence from Ash Park has Gino fearing that their relationship is over, but he is unaware that she is with Caroline awaiting the results of a pregnancy test. Meanwhile, James and Olivia continue to grow closer. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: New recruit Alex tries to get to the bottom of a local policeman's aggressive behaviour and is disturbed to discover he has a drug habit, while Richard edges ever closer to finding out the truth about Kate's affair with Andrew. Elsewhere, Tom sets the cat among the pigeons when he is forced to break some bad news. Medical drama, starring Gary Mavers and Maggie O'Neill

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The vets have to contend with an outbreak of illness on the grouse moors, and James tries to save a cow from slaughter. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison star

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: Hester and William rely on their son-in-law's knowledge of French law when they both find themselves in trouble with the authorities. Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers star

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom sets out to prove himself by learning to drive and Harvey again applies for membership of the golf club, masquerading as a clergyman. Sitcom set in the Bayview Retirement Village, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth feels harassed by a combination of working at a charity shop, trying to impress Councillor Nugent and worrying about Rose's love life. To cap it all, Daisy informs her that a strange woman in Daddy's bedroom claims to be his fiance. Starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: After helping Entwistle repair his truck, Truly, Clegg and Billy find their mechanical skills are put to the test once more when they help Tom and Smiler locate the source of an irritating squeak on their handcart. However, when Barry's longed-for game of golf evaporates thanks to his borrowed car breaking down, all the trio can do is help him drown his sorrows

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates a friend's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train, but everyone is initially stumped by the lack of a body. Feature-length drama, starring Joan Hickson, David Horovitch, Joanna David and Jill Meager

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The arrest of a Turkish girl who has been working illegally leads Ucos to reopen the case of Richard Gibson, a pub landlord who died in a fire in 2009. The original investigation concluded Gibson's death was probably suicide due to his dire financial situation, but the detectives have some questions about the pub's live-in barmaid, who disappeared on the night of the blaze. Could widowed landlady Joanne, pub regular Jason, or the dead man's best friend David, who happens to have a sideline in passing off his British-produced sparkling wine as French champagne, supply them with any answers? While the case tests the team's brain power, Sasha is also keen to raise their fitness levels as she signs them up for a five-a-side football competition. Phil Davis, Niamh Cusack and Tom Georgeson guest star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Part one of two. While Adam and Zaf hunt down an elite team of terrorists who have entered Britain, Ros is assigned to safeguard a delegation of Middle Eastern princes taking part in a secret government deal. However, things take a turn for the worse when the Saudi Trade Centre is stormed after news of the transaction is leaked. Rupert Penry-Jones and Raza Jaffrey star

programme.desc: Eleanor discovers a darker side to village life when there is a measles outbreak at the local school

programme.title: Bramwell

programme.desc: Dorien may have ulterior motives for inviting Tracey to a posh event, while Sharon makes a tidy profit out of fake Rolling Stones T-shirts. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Birds of a Feather

programme.title: The Bill

Donna sets to work single-handedly saving Christmas when the staff party is cancelled by hospital management

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Charlie is placed in an awkward position after discovering ex-wife Baz has remarried and her father has lung cancer, with very little time left to live. Simon breaks up with Tally, tension builds between Fin and Comfort, and Lara informs Harry she is not interested in anything more than friendship

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: John Heaton tells Gina the station faces the threat of closure and the team must pull together to keep Sun Hill on the map. New arrival Sgt Nikki Wright (Gillian Taylforth) is determined to show what she's made of, but fails to impress Roger, who complains to Doug Wright about her, not realising they are a married couple. Tony investigates an armed robbery at a jeweller's and a drug addict's body is discovered in a disused flat. A proposal from Rod Jessop stuns June

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark's gambling draws him into deeper debt. Alan finds ways of dealing with David Wicks. Nigel has words with Liam

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Albert Square is out in force for Arthur's pub-a-thon. The Jacksons have some good news, but the festivities turn sour when it seems all Arthur's hard work has been in vain

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Elizabeth returns from hospital and is unimpressed with the way Regina is behaving towards George and asks her to leave, and Olivia and James decide that the best chance for their family lies in the city. Romantic drama, starring Noni Hazlehurst

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew feels the wrath of health chief Richard Turner as news of his affair with Kate becomes common knowledge around Cardale _ and the Beeches staff find themselves caught in the crossfire. Things are particularly bad for Sam, whose efforts to support a rape victim are made doubly difficult, while Tom struggles to help a troubled teenager who is fighting a losing battle against anorexia. Popular drama, starring Gary Mavers, Maggie O'Neill and Joseph Millson

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: As part of an economy drive, Siegfried limits the use of an expensive brand of tablets, and Helen adopts a stray cat. The vets introduce new drugs for treating their clients' animals, but have to charge realistic prices for the services rendered. With Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: Hester lends a hand with a coach tour for British buyers of French homes, while William has plumbing worries. The pair are settling in well, but the good life turns sour when their daughter visits. Comedy, starring Julia Mackenzie and Anton Rodgers

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Diana watches calmly as Tom completes a bungee jump, and Harvey is informed that any preaching he does will be grounds for fraud charges. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Daisy tries to encourage Onslow to notice her by picking up a toy boy in the hope of making him jealous. Meanwhile, Hyacinth forces Elizabeth and Richard to help her organise a social occasion at the church hall. Patricia Routledge and Judy Cornwell star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: In the hope of avoiding a fuss, Clegg plans a quiet birthday lunch at a nice hotel - but his pals are sure to throw a few spanners in the works. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, from the pen of Roy Clarke. Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton star

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: Julia is assigned a compensation case brought by a man who has sustained spinal and brain damage in an accident at work, and tries to persuade Kavanagh to lead her. Unfortunately, he already has his hands full with a tricky compensation case and son Matt's attempts to get the better of him. John Thaw stars, with Ned Sherrin, Frances Tomelty and TP McKenna

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Steve McAndrew brings his dying father to London so he can spend his final days in a nearby hospice, but how much time he can spend with his parent is anyone's guess - the Ucos team is hard at work investigating the cold case murder of 55-year-old interpreter Agnes Bradley. A DNA sample provides a link to a rebellious teenager, and the team wonders if the killer could be related to him. But a whole new can of worms is opened when investigators discover the lad's mother was raped - could the same person have killed Agnes? Gerry and Steve hope that interviewing her friends from a local chess club may shed new light on the matter, but it's a possible professional issue that could hold the key to unlocking the mystery. Denis Lawson and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ros is trapped inside the embassy during the siege. The terrorists demand the release of al-Qaeda prisoners in Saudi Arabia and resort to killing the hostages when the government refuses to negotiate with them - but then suspicion arises as to the militants' real identities. Drama, starring Hermione Norris, with Saskia Reeves

programme.desc: A faith healer divides the Thrift into believers and non-believers, and Dr Marsham is particularly upset by his interference

programme.title: Bramwell

programme.title: Bramwell

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Struggling to deal with her feelings for both Sam and Kieron, Maria consults a clairvoyant. She is shown visions of her life in the past, present and future, which help her come to a decision. Since Faye is the only one offering her any assistance, Donna is missing the nurse's company as she prepares for the Christmas party and begins to doubt their friendship. Sam's bond with his newly discovered son is threatened by his awareness of Kieron's relationship with Maria, but, with everything out in the open, the surgeon realises where his priorities lie

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A birthday meal for Jim turns into a surprise engagement party for Nikki. Lara tells Harry she only sees him as a friend, and back at Holby, a man is admitted after a suffocation attempt by his partner, who can no longer cope with his deteriorating lung cancer. Gwyneth Strong and Tony Slattery guest star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Dan attempts heroics in a bid to rescue an 11-year-old from a canal boat where he is being held. The abductor turns out to be Rick Harris, whose addict son was found dead the previous day. June's private life gets entangled with public duties when she's called to boyfriend Rod's school to investigate one of his pupils - Kyle Henderson of the troublesome lottery-winning family - and there's a shock in store for her when the headmaster is critically injured in a hit-and-run incident

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pat comes down hard on Barry and David's dodgy deals. Ruth is determined to get to the bottom of Mark's problems. Neither Ricky nor David have the time for Bianca she would like

programme.title: Classic EastEnders

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George is delighted when Sarah agrees to marry him. When Elizabeth finds out about the proposal, she realises her threat to disinherit him has backfired - and decides to take a different approach. Elsewhere, Anna encounters complications with her pregnancy, and James and Olivia say goodbye to Harry before they leave Ash Park

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: A childless woman's bid to adopt is thrown into jeopardy by ill health and it's Sam who has to break the bad news. The burden of caring for baby Joe takes its toll on Tom, who resorts to finding a nanny to share the responsibility, and a recently widowed elderly gent announces to Andrew he feels he has nothing to live for

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The boxer dog Mrs Pumphrey bought to keep Tricky Woo company has a particularly embarrassing problem. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Margaretta Scott

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: In a bid to avoid the arrival of Emma's in-laws, William and Hester go on a rural camping trip, but manage to end up in Paris. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Jane and Diana's strip-club hen night proves to be a real eye-opener, then a double booking with a funeral at the church throws the happy couples' wedding plans into chaos. Comedy, starring Graham Crowden and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth attends the christening of Daisy's granddaughter, and is displeased to learn the child's parents are not married. With some encouragement from Richard, she agrees to keep an open mind, but is later irked when the mother and child go missing. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: After seeing a man skiing on a van roof, the pals are intrigued to discover it is Gavin Hinchcliffe, an old friend renowned for his eccentricity. Comedy series, guest starring Bernard Cribbins, with Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Josephine Tewson

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Anna Westin, a troubled childhood friend of Wallander's daughter, visits his house late at night begging for his help - but disappears soon afterwards. Meanwhile, an elderly woman's burned body is found in a shallow grave, and when the detective discovers a connection between Anna and the murder victim, he fears she could be the next target of a dangerous religious fanatic. Linda returns to Ystad to help with the investigation, and has life-changing news for her father. Guest starring Lindsay Duncan (Rome), with Kenneth Branagh and Jeany Spark

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An elderly amateur sleuth who was a friend of Danny's is murdered, and he ends up embroiled in the case when his photo is found at the scene. However, the cold case copper becomes convinced that the secret to uncovering her real killer lies within the last case she was working on. Picking up where she left off, he travels to the village of Minchampton, where a woman was said to have been murdered by a local lad 25 years previously. Meanwhile, Sasha is left to cope with the investigating officer on the modern-day Miss Marple's murder - an overbearing contemporary from her Hendon training, by the name of DCI Grace Mackie - and Steve lets Gerry stay with him when he is evicted, but soon comes to regret the act of kindness

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The team is given cause for concern when an extremist Muslim cleric, encouraging violence in the West, is apparently assassinated by a Christian zealot. This leads the MI5 agents to believe it could be the start of a new wave of religious conflicts. Rupert Penry-Jones, Peter Firth and Hermione Norris star, with Shaun Dingwall and Lennie James

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Dan attempts heroics in a bid to rescue an 11-year-old from a canal boat where he is being held. The abductor turns out to be Rick Harris, whose addict son was found dead the previous day. June's private life gets entangled with public duties when she's called to boyfriend Rod's school to investigate one of his pupils - Kyle Henderson of the troublesome lottery-winning family - and there's a shock in store for her when the headmaster is critically injured in a hit-and-run incident

programme.title: Rownd a Rownd
programme.desc: Jason's lack of enthusiasm is annoying Anest, and Carys makes a big decision that leaves Aled shocked. John persuades a nervous Sian to venture out but she does not cope well

programme.title: Newyddion S4C a'r Tywydd

programme.title: Antur Adre

programme.title: Tân Gwyllt
programme.desc: The different cultural aspects of fireworks, from their accidental discovery by Taoist monks in the sixth century to their association with extravaganza and spectacle. Plus, a look at the companies involved in artistic pyrotechnics, including one from Ruthin as it tries to set up a display on a rainy night by the river Conwy

programme.title: Am Dro!
programme.desc: A visit to the Dinefwr estate, the Faenol, the Cardiganshire Coastal Path by Llangrannog and the romantic island of Llanddwyn in Anglesey

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: A fly-tipper who illegally dumped tonnes of waste in the grounds of an Oldham primary school for children with learning difficulties, leaving the school with a £22,000 bill

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A teenager fights to save her father's life when he has a heart attack, and a driver loses control of a car, scooping two men up on to the bonnet and crashing through a shop window

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams search for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly. Presenter Anita Manning also delves into restoration by learning how to gild a bauble

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Deborah is left out in the cold, and Sid may be the only one who can help her. Daniel, Zara and Harper make an uneasy threesome, and Bear tries to help a friend in need

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Presenter Steve Brown helps a bachelor find an ideal property in Guildford, Surrey, on a budget of £420,000

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and the team are at Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, where they transform a nest of tables, a tired trolley, and a telephone table

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items brought in by members of the public, including a selection of Hornby model railway equipment. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jay is amazed with Honey's strength after she talks to the police again, while Mick is unimpressed when Max offers Linda another shift at the restaurant. Kat comes up with a brainwave to sort out the Slaters' money woes - but Stacey and Kush are not best pleased with the idea. Tiffany is overwhelmed with the more morbid aspects of her new job at the funeral parlour but Rainie and Stuart do their best to make her feel welcome as only they can

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Ten well-known actors come together to record an album for Children in Need. Cameras follow Helena Bonham Carter, Jim Broadbent, Olivia Colman, Shaun Dooley, Luke Evans, Suranne Jones, Adrian Lester, Himesh Patel, David Tennant and Jodie Whittaker over 12 weeks in the summer of 2019 as they rehearse songs for their album, Got It Covered. Each actor must choose a song that has special meaning for them and must make their cover version their own. The participants also visit some charity projects that Children in Need supports in order to help raise funds and awareness

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: Hannah's wedding day arrives, bringing everyone's stories together. David explores memories of his own marriage, Belle makes an unlikely alliance, Hannah confronts the question that has been plaguing her ever since Andy came back into her life, and Gail realises she may be the only one who can help

programme.title: Catch Her If You Can
programme.desc: Documentary following an attempt to expose a successful London businesswoman's double life as a con artist. A suspicious invoice tipped off one of her potential victims, and inspired her to track down other parties who had been fleeced by her in the past. Together, they came up with a plan to expose her misdeeds

programme.title: Rent Like a Boss
programme.desc: Big V and Tobias give their advice on outrageous rental issues, from dealing with nosy neighbours to landlords who do not respect their tenants' privacy. They also help a Manchester resident looking for a flatshare, who insists on bringing a caravan full of theatrical costumes with him to his new home

programme.title: US Election 2020
programme.desc: Katty Kay and Andrew Neil present rolling coverage of the US election result, as either Donald Trump or Joe Biden will be confirmed as president. Trump and vice president Mike Pence are hoping to hold onto the positions they have held for the Republican Party since the last election in 2016, while Barack Obama's former VP Biden and running mate Kamala Harris are looking to regain Democratic control of the White House

programme.title: US Election 2020
programme.desc: Katty Kay and Andrew Neil present rolling coverage of the US election result

programme.title: US Election 2020
programme.desc: Katty Kay and Andrew Neil present rolling coverage of the US election results.

programme.title: US Election 2020
programme.desc: Matthew Amroliwala and Reeta Chakrabarti with further coverage of the US election results

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Deborah tries to build bridges and brings the team together in more ways than one. Meanwhile, Zara meets a patient who is a little too familiar

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin offers advice to house hunters who want to move to the lively market town of Abergavenny in Monmouthshire

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey, Sarah Moore comes across items to salvage and sell on for a profit, including a retro bureau and two unloved chairs

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to make an offer on a collection of Matchbox toy cars, a collection of silver-plated goblets, a snuff box, and a model giraffe taken from a carousel

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Amol Rajan

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville's idea to open a new catering arm, offering his customers tasty toasties, doesn't quite go according to plan when his reconditioned toaster blows a fuse. Mr Newbold gets a little bolder in his pursuit of Mrs Rossi, while trying to avoid the Black Widow. Leroy and Willis meet Beth and Ruby with a view to going on a date, but Willis is worried that Ruby's not right for him. David Jason, James Baxter, Johnny Vegas and Geoffrey Whitehead star in veteran writer Roy Clarke's much-loved comedy

programme.title: Children in Need: 40 Fabulous Years
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan takes viewers on a funny, poignant and revealing tour across four decades of Children in Need

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Incident Response Officer Andy is dispatched when a call comes in about a reported stabbing, while Adam and Orani are dispatched to treat a long-term heroin addict whose concerned mother has called the ambulance service for help. Elsewhere, Kieran and Mick are dispatched to their second patient of the night, an 82-year-old lady who has fallen at home and has been waiting for an ambulance for two hours

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: The contestants are challenged to collaborate with each other, before facing off in a fearsome clash as they hope to impress DJ Target, Krept and Konan, with guests Yungen and Young T and Bugsey

programme.title: Rap Trip: Underground Scenes Uncovered
programme.desc: FOS and Ransom travel to the Republic of Ireland to discover the underground scene, where they pay a visit to teenage viral sensations Cal and Pepper in Tallaght

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness introduces Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff to his latest creation - an all-electric, all-terrain ice-cream van for the 21st century. Elsewhere, the team tests the battery-powered Honda E, Vauxhall Corsa-e and Mini Electric with a race around a deserted Alton Towers

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The progress of a three-bedroom semi in Crewe, Cheshire, a studio apartment in London's Hammersmith and a mid-terrace in Colne, Lancashire - all of which are bought as investments

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Officers investigate a travel company boss conning customers. They also examine a sewage leak which polluted a stream running through an Oxfordshire village

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A young boy rushes for help after his grandfather falls down a cliff face, a road racing cyclist's career is threatened when he is hit by a car and a motorbike rider hits black ice

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from Newmarket Racecourse, Suffolk, where two teams of best friends go head to head to maximise their returns, assisted by experts John Cameron and Caroline Hawley. Anita also learns about a legend of horse racing who didn't set foot on the turf

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara convinces Al to help her fix Harper, while Daniel and Bear try to find a new nurse for the Mill. Emma helps a mother and daughter get a new start

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Steve Brown heads to Bideford in Devon to show his buyers a few properties for their £435,000, while also taking in community life, from its busy arts scene to its luxury ice-cream

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore gives an old tartan picnic rug to bag-maker Rosanna Gethin and a set of tired chairs to upcycler Rachel Henderson, while she restyles a vintage glass display cabinet

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including pieces of pottery saved from a tip, two unusual fire screens and a mirror. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Amol Rajan

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Keegan accidentally knocks Rainie over on his bike, forcing her to admit she is pregnant to a horrified Tiffany and Keegan. Kat is annoyed that Phil turned down her offer, and while Kush suggests it's for the best, she refuses to let the idea drop. Linda arranges a medication review for Mick, who is furious that she has gone behind her back, and Jay has good news about a flat he and Lola were hoping to move in to - but she does not share his enthusiasm

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin help Aliyah and Ajaz, who have two young children, making mealtimes hard work. To make matters worse, the couple have differing tastes, so they rarely eat the same meal. And when time is against them, the takeaway wins yet again. Can Gregg and Chris help change their costly ways? Plus, Chris visits a chilli farm in Devon to try the world's hottest chilli and a team of tennis players taste test five lemonades

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The trip down Apprentice memory lane concludes with a look back at what it takes for tasks to be a soaring success - and how to avoid an epic failure. There are jaw-dropping lows, including nauseating food failures and cringe-inducing pitches, as well as the best of the best - from TV selling superstars to those who clean up on dirty jobs. Alan Sugar, Karren Brady and Claude Littner reminisce about the best and worst moments as the candidates fought to avoid the taxi of shame

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker hosts the light-hearted quiz, with Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell heading teams of celebrities in a test of their sporting knowledge. Taking part this time around are horse racing legend AP McCoy, World Cup-winning cricketer Charlotte Edwards, former England goalkeeper David James and Olympic cycling champion Joanna Rowsell Shand

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and Danny Sebastian help two teams hunt for antiques at the West Midlands Showground in Shrewsbury, while Natasha Raskin Sharp tests the design knowledge of fairgoers

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades transforms a stained-glass door and window, Rachel Henderson takes an old fireplace, and Mark Haig attempts to inject new life into a collection of brass lighting

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don gives tips on how to look after wildlife in the months ahead, shows how to look after and protect tender garden plants and plans ahead for next year's harvest of fruit. Joe Swift finds out what it takes to look after a historic garden with a collection of heritage fruit trees in Hampshire, and Arit Anderson meets Fergus Garrett - head gardener at Great Dixter - to discover how the planting and garden management have increased biodiversity. Plus, the Staffordshire couple with a particular fondness for Japanese maples

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Wigan Warriors and Hull Kingston Rovers v Hull FC

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing's Len Goodman takes Baroness Tanni Grey-Thompson on a journey to Somerset to recreate a childhood holiday of 1980. The pair travel in a bright yellow Rover, just like her family car back in the day, and they visit the Cameley Lakes where the former Paralympian would have stayed. They also visit Longleat Safari park, go fishing, and visit the Wells Cathedral

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Weld receives another unexpected visitor, and George is convinced that Big Mac and Terry's new restaurant is changing the character of the town

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the six remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room to revive the glamorous, and sometimes ridiculous style, of the 1980s. Patrick Grant and Esme Young challenge the sewers to get down to business with the trickiest pattern so far, a woman's power jacket, before they are tasked with turning high-vis workman clothes into a party outfit in the Transformation Challenge. Finally in the Made to Measure Challenge, the contestants create their own versions of a cocktail dress

programme.title: Britain and the Sea
programme.desc: David Dimbleby continues his voyage around Britain, sailing his yacht Rocket along coastal regions of the South East, the first point of attack for enemy forces and therefore essential to the nation's defence throughout history. Along the way, he takes a tour of HMS Victory, Nelson's flagship vessel from the Battle of Trafalgar, lovingly preserved in dry dock in Portsmouth. He also meets a carpenter who still makes wooden figureheads, discovers how a chamber pot acted as propaganda in the battle against Napoleon and reveals the way abstract art was used to deceive German submarine pilots

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mark Billingham, Neil Delamere, AJ Odudu and Lucy Porter test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared each Friday

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball chats to Jamie Laing and Karen Hauer, the lucky couple who survived the weekend's dance-off, and there is a closer look at the weekend's routines. Plus, more interviews and backstage gossip

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Radio 2's Janey Lee Grace and Tim Smith travel around the Cotswolds and the Malverns in search of antiques, assisted by experts James Braxton and David Harper. Janey's vintage Austin Healey takes her to uncover the story of cross-dressing music-hall superstar Vesta Tilley, while sports fan Tim - in a sporty MG - gets to grips with the art of Shin Kicking and the 'Cotswold Olympicks'

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham reports live on badger and fox families in the New Forest, while Michaela Strachan catches up with the latest from a seal breeding colony on the Isle of May. Iolo Williams goes in search of a wild parrot-like bird in a secret location in west Wales, and Gillian Burke discovers the surprising wildlife to be found amongst the chimney stacks and industrial ponds of South Yorkshire

programme.title: Aung San Suu Kyi: The Fall of an Icon
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the Myanmar leader's fall from grace. While under house arrest during the country's military dictatorship, she became a Nobel prize-winning champion of democracy, human rights and peaceful protest. However, since becoming the leader of her nation, she has been implicated in brutal atrocities against the Rohingya Muslims, and faced allegations of genocide at the International Court of Justice. Key figures in international politics discuss the reasons why the qualities that brought her such acclaim have been in marked contrast to the realities of her leadership

programme.title: Out of Her Mind
programme.desc: Sara organises a baby shower for Scoopy to prove she does not make everything about her, and is visited by a ghostly reminder of her own pregnancy

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: After advertising her home on Airbnb, Mandy takes in a guest from Russia - and they soon become more than friends. But can he be trusted? Comedy, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with Tony Way (After Life)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Travelling Blind
programme.desc: Amar Latif is blind but that has never prevented him from travelling the world, having now visited more than a hundred countries and taken up skydiving, bungee jumping and skiing. But whenever Amar travels he relies on a sighted companion to guide him. In return he opens their eyes and their senses to a different way of travelling. In this one-off film, he is joined by comedian Sara Pascoe on a trip to Turkey, intending to go off the beaten track and explore the country's remote mountains and extraordinary mountains. However, it soon becomes clear they have very different ways of travelling and the journey becomes as much about their relationship as it is about the country

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Laurence asks Kris to fight in a charity boxing match to raise cash for a homeless shelter. Can he make the leap from mild-mannered hotel manager to prizefighter? VIP guest Louise Brookes checks in, asking for the same room she stayed in 175 times while she attended the inquest into the Hillsborough disaster, and Ciara launches a new competition to promote weddings at the hotel

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: Harry Hill casts a comical eye over the history of police dramas, from the early days of Dixon of Dock Green to acclaimed modern shows like Line of Duty. He examines the familiar tropes that audiences know and love, as well as drawing attention to a few that have escaped audience's attention

programme.title: The Trump Show
programme.desc: Key players including Rudy Giuliani, Stormy Daniels, Steve Bannon and Ambassador John Bolton shed light on Trump's second year, as he faces challenges from his past

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Special episode looking at knife crime in the UK, including a dedicated police task force in Essex and a groundbreaking scheme in the West Midlands

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A young man lies unconscious in his bedroom and it is up to an on-line friend in Texas to raise the alarm. Plus, a nine-year-old boy on holiday is stung by a giant jellyfish

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A teenager fights to save her father's life when he has a heart attack, and a driver loses control of a car, scooping two men up on to the bonnet and crashing through a shop window

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and the team are at Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, where they transform a nest of tables, a tired trolley, and a telephone table

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Yvonne Cobb and Julian Peedle-Calloo learn about a deaf adventurer on a mission to complete all the Munro tops in Scotland. With voiceover

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: JB Gill takes a stroll along the river, from the Kent Downs, through chocolate-box villages, the cathedral city of Canterbury and on towards the port of Sandwich and Pegwell Bay. Along the way, he visits a grand house that is pictured on the £10 note, views a giant crown carved in chalk on a hillside and finds a section of the river walk designed specifically for wheelchair users

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Helen and Simon Holroyd sample life in Australia for a week to find out if a move there might help save their relationship, which is under strain from Simon's heavy work schedule. Nicki Chapman presents

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development of a terrace in Bamford, Lancashire, a three-storey building in Lower Gornal in the West Midlands and a flat in the Kent town of Gravesend

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from Epsom Down Racecourse in Surrey, where the reds and blues go head to head to make as much profit as possible. Danny Sebastian and David Harper look after the bargain hunters who have to spend £300 on three items within an hour in the hope they'll make loads of money at the auction in Maidenhead, Berkshire

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter H. Tony Allan and Giorgio Locatelli cook ham bone soup and Jamie Oliver makes a pine nut and honey tart

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: The Strictly Dome Dancing judge takes singer, actress and presenter Toyah Willcox back to the Llangollen Canal in north Wales to reminisce about a narrowboat holiday she enjoyed there in 1968. Toyah reveals how her time on the canal was very different to that spent in her home city of Birmingham, symbolising freedom and fun. She and Len tuck into a vintage picnic and visit an antiques shop like the ones Toyah's father would have taken her to while away from home

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George's article about the restaurant causes outrage in town and he worries about Hannah, who has been defending Terry since the beginning of their romance

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the five remaining contestants take part in the quarter-final, creating garments and trying their hand at techniques from around the world. Firstly, the sewers must follow a pattern like they have never encountered before, as judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a Filipino Terno Blouse. In the Transformation Challenge, the competitors are asked to turn Provencal tablecloths from France into a brand new outfit in just 90 minutes, before creating a dramatic Flamenco skirt from Southern Spain in the final challenge

programme.title: Big Cats About the House
programme.desc: Following the work of big cat expert Giles Clark, as he embarks on a mission to transform the Big Cat Sanctuary in Kent into a centre of global excellence for conservation. In the opening episode, Giles provides around the clock care to Maya, a five-day-old black jaguar cub neglected by her mother. Then, he travels to Cambodia to witness the dilemma clouded leopards are facing in the wild

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Revisiting some of the historic venues from the series, and Paul Martin also finds out all about the fascinating people of Sledmere house through the ages

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mark Billingham, Neil Delamere, AJ Odudu and Lucy Porter test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball gives all the backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing, and Ian Waite gives his thoughts on the couples' training progress.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The Rev Richard Coles and actor Joe McFadden scour Scotland for antiques with David Harper and Philip Serrell, with a nailbiting auction ensuing after both teams buy near-identical items. Before the auction south of the border in Newcastle, Richard and David play football at one of Scotland's most famous clubs, while Joe and Philip end up going round in circles deep underground

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams all look at the unseen side of autumn from their four separate locations across the country. Tonight, the team share some big news about hedgehogs, and also looks at one of the cutest and strangest marine predators, the bobtail squid

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Period drama set in London in the 1760s following Margaret Wells as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner. Charlotte and Isaac find they have an irresistible attraction for each other. Meanwhile, Emily persuades the Pinchers to make an investment, but she is shocked when asked to help seal the deal. Samantha Morton and Lesley Manville star

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits the Portuguese city of Lisbon, where he is inspired by the city's love of seafood to cook his own take on salt cod fritters

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: A light-hearted look at the social and cultural changes society went through since the turn of the 21st century, featuring contributions from politicians and celebrities. In the third episode, Angela Scanlon, Kimberley Walsh and Dane Baptiste take a light-hearted look back at the year 2002, from Justin Timberlake's clothing to the Spooks television series

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Alex Brooker: Disability and Me
programme.desc: The Last Leg presenter confronts what disability really means in Britain today. Alex is reunited with a childhood friend who has spina bifida, who challenges him to consider the impact of making jokes about his own disability. He also pays a visit to a bespoke disability helpline, meets members of Arsenal's disabled supporters club, and talks to Paralympic swimmer Susie Rodgers about her life

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Yvonne Cobb and Julian Peedle-Calloo learn about a deaf adventurer on a mission to complete all the Munro tops in Scotland. With voiceover

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor and author David Walliams delves into his family history, learning of his paternal great-grandfather's traumatic experiences during the First World War and unearthing the story of an ancestor who became blind as the result of pioneering eye surgery in the 1880s

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to Cyprus on a budget rental car adventure and sample the exciting new sport of car water-skiing. Back at the test track, Chris and Paddy check out the latest family cars from Audi and Lamborghini

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore a quirky cottage in Paddock Wood, Kent, a new build in Gnosall, Staffordshire, and a one-bedroom flat in Northampton

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey, Sarah Moore comes across items to salvage and sell on for a profit, including a retro bureau and two unloved chairs

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming: Compilation
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of High Court enforcement officers, who strive to retrieve money for others. Rob and Gerald brave the red carpet at a celebrity hotspot in London to try to recover the unpaid wages of a former worker. There is an angry confrontation in a London motorcycle shop when agents try to remove goods to collect a debt owed to a customer, and Michael and Jess help a man who was forced out of his job while recovering from cancer. Can they get him the large sum of money a tribunal awarded him?

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Author and former MP Edwina Currie relives a childhood holiday in Bournemouth from 1957, in the company of Len Goodman. Along the way, they talk about her life as a student, her Orthodox Jewish upbringing and the inevitable controversies brought about by her time in parliament

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Zak, Ike and Steve are missing at sea and the town mounts a huge search-and-rescue operation. Antipodean comedy drama, starring Erik Thomson

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The semi-final sees the hopefuls creating garments inspired by silver screen. First, they must follow a pattern like none they have encountered before, as judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a dress inspired by Marilyn Monroe's in The Seven Year Itch. In the Transformation Challenge, the sewers have to make a sci-fi inspired outfit in just 90 minutes, before creating a made to measure flapper dress for the final task of the semi final

programme.title: Big Cats About the House
programme.desc: Giles Clark is feeling overwhelmed by the enormity of the task he has taken on with the Big Cat Sanctuary in Kent. He is paying close attention to the health of Maya the jaguar, who is now seven weeks old, in particular her wavering eyesight and back legs that still seem a little wobbly. Giles also visits Costa Rica to help jaguars in the wild

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The experts search for items at Penrhyn Castle in north Wales, where David Harper looks at vintage Second World War posters and Claire Rawle is surprised by a valuable watch

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mark Billingham, Neil Delamere, AJ Odudu and Lucy Porter test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal dishes more backstage gossip and raids the rails with costume designer Vicky Gill to find out what some of the couples will be wearing this weekend

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Powering Britain
programme.desc: Keeley Donovan goes behind the scenes of Drax Power Station, 14 miles from York in North Yorkshire, and the biggest power station in Britain. She follows engineers as they undertake a £50million maintenance programme that requires them to move equipment weighing 300 tons. There are also giant boilers and turbines to refurbish as they get one of their six generating units re-connected to the National Grid

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: The team focus on the incredible journeys made by wildlife in autumn. Megan McCubbin catches up with rare whales in the Scottish Highlands, Iolo Williams reveals the science behind nocturnal mass migration, Chris Packham and Megan light up the New Forest to visualise secret fungal networks, and Gillian Burke learns how ducks support their own team colours

programme.title: Being Frank: The Frank Gardner Story
programme.desc: The BBC security correspondent explores what it is like to suddenly become disabled, something he experienced himself 16 years ago after being shot by Al-Qaeda gunmen in Saudi Arabia. The bullets damaged his spinal nerves, leaving him partially paralysed and he has used a wheelchair ever since. In this deeply personal film, he talks candidly about the effects his injuries have had on his life, work, relationships and the way he views himself. And he speaks to others, to explore how they have responded to sudden life-changing injuries

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Maisie Adam, Ed Byrne, Ed Gamble, Sukh Ojla and Mark Simmons. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Highlights from the satirical news show, presented by Nish Kumar, as a cast of comedy correspondents give their take on the events from May 2019

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events with Emily Maitlis and Emma Barnett

programme.title: Birds of Paradise: The Ultimate Quest
programme.desc: Part one of two. Thirty years ago explorer Benedict Allen lived in Papua New Guinea with the Niowra, a remote people. Broadcast journalist Frank Gardner has always wished to see wild birds of paradise, so Benedict resolves to take him along. The duo set out through some of the most inhospitable terrain on the planet, negotiating swamps, mountains and crocodile-infested waters, heading into the cloud forest. Despite Frank requiring the use of a wheelchair following a shooting incident in 2004, Benedict determines to get him to their destination

programme.title: Birds of Paradise: The Ultimate Quest
programme.desc: Part two of two. Explorer Benedict Allen is determined to get disabled journalist Frank Gardner into the wilds of Papua New Guinea, despite having to grapple with Frank's wheelchair. As the terrain gets even tougher to negotiate, the pair know they must make an epic journey into the highlands, crossing through two tribal territories to achieve their objective. However, as Frank gets close to realising his dream of seeing wild birds of paradise up-close, his old injuries return to haunt him and the expedition hangs on a knife edge

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin recalls the making of his third travel series Full Circle, a journey around the Pacific Rim which took him to Russia, China, Vietnam, Australia and New Zealand and North and South America. This series saw Michael adopting a more investigative role than in previous odysseys, particularly when encountering the drug wars and poverty that plagued Colombia. With contributions from David Attenborough and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: In the Face of Terror
programme.desc: The live-streaming of an attack on mosques in Christchurch, New Zealand, in March 2019 heralds a new era of terrorism and confirms the far right as a fast-growing threat. Survivor Abdul Aziz describes how he was praying when a fellow worshipper was shot through a window, and he ran outside to confront the attacker, while analyst Jacob Davey recalls monitoring the digital footprint of the attack

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.desc: Return of the documentary that goes behind the scenes on the most complex murder and large-scale criminal investigations in Scotland. The detectives investigate the case of 37-year-old Paul Mathieson, who was assaulted and found fighting for his life on a street in Renfrew. The victim of a brutal assault, Paul succumbs to his horrific injuries, and the investigators of Police Scotland's Major Investigations Team take up the case

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: Visits to historic places of work, including one of Britain's oldest dockyards in Chatham Kent, and Armley Mill in Leeds. At a 1,000-year-old tithe barn in Sussex, the steeplejack tries his hand at thatching, before getting a surprise on the top floor of the architecturally esteemed Lloyd's building in the City of London

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints a majestic snow-covered mountain, complete with rolling foothills, leafy trees, bushes and a serene lake

programme.title: Vikings
programme.desc: Neil Oliver tells the story of how the age of the Vikings came to an end, using archaeological evidence to explore the lives of the first Danish kings, the spread of Christianity in Scandinavia, and the voyages of discovery undertaken by Norse sailors in the Atlantic. He also profiles Canute, the former king of England, and reveals why the reputation he has accrued over the past millennium does a disservice to his diplomatic feats

programme.title: Blood of the Clans
programme.desc: When Bonnie Prince Charlie arrives in Scotland to regain the crown of his ancestors, the clans are called upon to rise in armed revolt for him. Many do, and they invade England, but others oppose him and join the British side to fight against the rebels. Some though play a cunning double game, pretending to be loyal to the British whilst secretly raising clansmen for the Prince. Presented by Neil Oliver

programme.title: Play for Today
programme.desc: Female workers at a textile factory clash with their own union. Drama by Colin Welland, starring Lynne Perrie and Elizabeth Spriggs. Originally broadcast in 1974

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: The Minna case is reopened after Rolf and Neel find serious errors in the Danish DNA register, and Rolf and his former boss Astrid Oxlev assist the French police - with Claire Bobain (Charlotte Rampling) the lead investigator. In Danish, English, Polish and French

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: With clues pointing to Poland, the team heads there to find a crucial witness in possession of a video, but Claire is concerned that Rolf seems more focused on his daughter's case than with the murder they are currently investigating. In Danish, English, Polish and French

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints a majestic snow-covered mountain, complete with rolling foothills, leafy trees, bushes and a serene lake

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The former steeplejack explores the achievements of Britain's civil engineering pioneers, visiting the Channel Tunnel, Humber Bridge and Pontcysyllte Aqueduct. He also has a go at legging a narrowboat through a canal tunnel in Dudley in the West Midlands

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as he paints a scene depicting a cloudy sky above a turbulent seascape of crashing waves

programme.title: New York: America's Busiest City
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan, Anita Rani, Ant Anstead and Dan Snow take a closer look at one of the most complex and powerful cities in the world, focusing on transport links in the first edition. Anita, Ade and Ant head to Grand Central Terminal, joining the daily commute on the iconic Staten Island ferry as well as the subway, suburban railroad and citibikes. Dan visits Times Square to see how the 230 LED advertising hoardings are maintained, while Ade takes a ride in a yellow cab, discovering that the business model is under threat from newly created taxi apps

programme.title: The Disordered Eye
programme.desc: Disabled artist and film-maker Richard Butchins questions whether good vision equals great art, and looks at modern and historical artists through the lens of their impaired vision

programme.title: CripTales
programme.desc: Fictional monologues based on factual research and the lived experienced of disabled people in Britain. In 1968, wheelchair user Sue finds herself pregnant after a whirlwind romance, and now has to decide whether she wants to go ahead with a newly-legalised abortion

programme.title: CripTales
programme.desc: Fictional monologues based on factual research and the lived experienced of disabled people in Britain. Hamish, equipped with a new, self-operated wheelchair, heads for the woods to find sexual pleasure, but he had not counted on such uneven terrain

programme.title: Eugenics: Science's Greatest Scandal
programme.desc: Part one of two. Science journalist Angela Saini and disability rights activist Adam Pearson, reveal that eugenics - the controversial idea that was a driving force behind the Nazi death camps - originated in the upper echelons of the British scientific community. programme.title: Eugenics: Science's Greatest Scandal
programme.desc: Concluding the two-part documentary exploring the shocking story of eugenics, the controversial idea that the human race can be improved by selective breeding. It is commonly thought that the idea disappeared after the horrors of the Nazi Holocaust, but as science journalist Angela Saini and disability rights activist Adam Pearson discover, practices such as the sterilisation of the poor continued on a global scale for many more years

programme.desc: Bing and Sula play a vooshing game with their favourite toys, Hoppity Voosh and Hippo Fair, and they throw them into the air to see how high they can fly

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos try wrapping using bandages

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin Fletcher spends the day at his friend's school while Mr Tumble plays 'school' with the toys

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies go on a very special picnic together. Lots of Tubby Toast pops out of the Dup Dup and lands on the picnic plates for the Teletubbies to munch

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Enid the cat is stuck up the tree, and Duggee and the squirrels must think of a way to get her down

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: The monsters bring their toys to school and everyone wants to play with Icklewoo's robot Whizz-bang

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: Sid Sloane steps through the Magic Curtain to become a painter and decorator, and is employed to help a team transform one of the biggest rooms in Lord Poshington's huge home

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: The little boy's tummy makes funny noises. Noa thinks he might have swallowed something odd. They realise it is his tummy, but nobody knows what it is trying to say

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Ottalie's friend Noah comes to play. They spend the morning making an outside den with big sticks and blankets. Mum and little sister Imogen join them in the den for lunch. They also get creative and make a giant picture using lots of paint and sand

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: A boy pushes Tig by accident at nursery school, leaving her scared to go back. However, Woolly tells her the importance of sharing her worries

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: The Leafs take refuge in the Olobobs's home, but like it so much that they won't leave

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: A young boy whose family hails from Israel bakes challa bread, which is normally only eaten on special occasions

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle and Mrs Hobbs are poorly. Waffle has to rest while the Brooklyn-Bells do chores for Mrs Hobbs to help her get better. Jess brings home a surprise from work

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Otus and Timmy make a kite, but the lamb's excitement and impatience cause him to destroy it, spoiling all the hard work his friend put in

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Katie and Liam are looking for a large piece of paper to do their next big painting on, but when their parents are too busy in the Post Office to help and Grannie Island is unable to assist, the feisty seven-year-old calls at the Lady Artist's house in the hope she will know where to get one from

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: Mum has a surprise for Melody when they enjoy a day out at the seaside, and they have a go on the carousel

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee is heartbroken when he can't find his favourite toy, Action Ape, and goes straight to Mo for help. Mo is more interested in helping Tee learn to look for things. Where did you last see him, Tee?

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: G says she can grow wishes, but U does not believe in them

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Deed encourages a friend to say ‘cow' and we go on a whirlwind word adventure

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Three opens a stampoline park, where her friends have splatty fun making inky prints of all the shapes they can make. Learn to recognise amounts on sight with the Numberblocks

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Charlie upsets Bing when he doesn't want to play the games he planned, but the pair soon find that making a noise in the kitchen is lots of fun

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: When Messy finds a dusty sock underneath his bed, he goes to Okido to find out why it makes him teary

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin spends the day with a radio star at her school. They record a rap for CBeebies Radio. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble and Grandad Tumble run Tumble FM Radio Show

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The playful quartet hear a 'honk honk' in the distance as the Tubby Car approaches. The Tubby Car drives the Teletubbies across Teletubbyland as they sing the Tubby Car song

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: But while he is showing them what he can do, he accidentally rubs them out! Pug helps the pencil get them back to their original design.

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: With Ferne Corrigan and Rory Crawford

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Four-year-old Ottalie lives on a farm, and heads out with her father to count and feed the sheep. It's a foggy day and the sheep are hard to find but they soon appear when they know it's food time

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Cook creates the most delicious soup anyone has ever tasted, and Captain Captain orders him to make more so they can sell it and become rich

Can Gregg and Chris help change their costly ways? Plus, Chris visits a chilli farm in Devon to try the world's hottest chilli and a team of tennis players taste test five lemonades

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The trip down Apprentice memory lane concludes with a look back at what it takes for tasks to be a soaring success - and how to avoid an epic failure. There are jaw-dropping lows, including nauseating food failures and cringe-inducing pitches, as well as the best of the best - from TV selling superstars to those who clean up on dirty jobs. Alan Sugar, Karren Brady and Claude Littner reminisce about the best and worst moments as the candidates fought to avoid the taxi of shame

programme.desc: Brian Cunningham and his children Ben and Eilidh make wildlife flower balls and a runner bean tepee

programme.title: First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Kirsten Campbell presents live coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara convinces Al to help her fix Harper, while Daniel and Bear try to find a new nurse for the Mill. Emma helps a mother and daughter get a new start

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Steve Brown heads to Bideford in Devon to show his buyers a few properties for their £435,000, while also taking in community life, from its busy arts scene to its luxury ice-cream

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore gives an old tartan picnic rug to bag-maker Rosanna Gethin and a set of tired chairs to upcycler Rachel Henderson, while she restyles a vintage glass display cabinet

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including pieces of pottery saved from a tip, two unusual fire screens and a mirror. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Amol Rajan

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Keegan accidentally knocks Rainie over on his bike, forcing her to admit she is pregnant to a horrified Tiffany and Keegan. Kat is annoyed that Phil turned down her offer, and while Kush suggests it's for the best, she refuses to let the idea drop. Linda arranges a medication review for Mick, who is furious that she has gone behind her back, and Jay has good news about a flat he and Lola were hoping to move in to - but she does not share his enthusiasm

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin help Aliyah and Ajaz, who have two young children, making mealtimes hard work. To make matters worse, the couple have differing tastes, so they rarely eat the same meal. And when time is against them, the takeaway wins yet again. Can Gregg and Chris help change their costly ways? Plus, Chris visits a chilli farm in Devon to try the world's hottest chilli and a team of tennis players taste test five lemonades

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The trip down Apprentice memory lane concludes with a look back at what it takes for tasks to be a soaring success - and how to avoid an epic failure. There are jaw-dropping lows, including nauseating food failures and cringe-inducing pitches, as well as the best of the best - from TV selling superstars to those who clean up on dirty jobs. Alan Sugar, Karren Brady and Claude Littner reminisce about the best and worst moments as the candidates fought to avoid the taxi of shame

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker hosts the light-hearted quiz, with Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell heading teams of celebrities in a test of their sporting knowledge. Taking part this time around are horse racing legend AP McCoy, World Cup-winning cricketer Charlotte Edwards, former England goalkeeper David James and Olympic cycling champion Joanna Rowsell Shand
